evidence.scientific_clarity: Architecture diagram clearly shows RetroDiff pipeline with Retrieval (Retriever+KB) and Generation (Diffusion Decoder+Prior) groups; solid vs dashed arrow semantics correctly encode data flow vs control/feedback per brief.
evidence.typography: Script does not set pdf.fonttype=42 or rcParams; PDF embeds Type3 fonts (DejaVu Sans Bold, Oblique, Regular) — not selectable/editable TrueType; default DejaVu Sans font family used throughout.
evidence.palette: Uses blue/green/yellow/gray — no red-green confusion; palette is mostly colorblind-safe, but no explicit CB-safe palette chosen (e.g. no ColorBrewer/okabe-ito); group shading and node colors are visually distinct.
evidence.layout_density: Figure occupies panel space well; node positions are well-spread with no overlapping labels; legend fits cleanly at bottom; whitespace is balanced, not gaping.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and explains arrow semantics; legend is functional but uses colored patch proxies that don't distinguish solid from dashed lines clearly; no numerical claim (appropriate for architecture diagram).
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type3 bitmap-embedded fonts (not Type42); text is NOT selectable in PDF; script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42 or backend_pdf settings.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial; SVG is an additional bonus artifact.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption identifies system name, explains solid/dashed arrow semantics, describes both groups with their constituent nodes, and names the two specific dashed arrows with their functional meaning — thorough for an architecture figure.

overall_strengths: Clear, well-structured architecture diagram that faithfully represents the brief's pipeline with correct group boxes, node types, and arrow style conventions.
All required output files are present (PDF, PNG, SVG, caption, script) and the caption is detailed and accurate, explaining each architectural element.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 is not set, so the PDF embeds Type3 (bitmap) fonts instead of TrueType — text is not selectable or editable in the PDF, failing the vector fidelity requirement.

Default DejaVu Sans font is used with no colorblind-safe palette explicitly chosen; legend patch proxies do not clearly show the solid vs dashed line distinction (patch fill color differences are subtle).
